pi_items.source: setup_task.sh line 46 creates product with --name="Classic T-Shirt"; export_result.sh line 21 queries this product name
setup_task.sh lines 34-42 explicitly ensure Black term does not exist initially; export_result.sh line 25 checks for Black term creation; task.json description confirms Black is the new color
setup_task.sh creates Red and Blue with CTS-RED and CTS-BLU SKUs (lines 55-56); task.json description specifies 'CTS-BLK' for Black; verifier.py line 32 confirms expected_sku
setup_task.sh lines 55-56 create Red and Blue variations at 20.00; task.json description explicitly states Black variation should be 25.00; verifier.py line 33 confirms expected_price

setup_task.sh lines 55-56 both use --regular_price="20.00" for Red and Blue; task.json description notes 'Standard colors are $20.00'; verifier.py line 89 integrity check confirms Red variation is 20.0

privileged_info_summary: The expand_product_variations task modifies a WooCommerce variable product named 'Classic T-Shirt'. The task requires adding a new 'Black' color option (which is initially excluded from the product's attributes) to create a complete product variation offering. The new Black variation must have a higher price point ($25.00) compared to existing standard colors (Red and Blue at $20.00 base price). The Black variation must be assigned the SKU 'CTS-BLK', following the naming convention of existing variations (CTS-RED, CTS-BLU). All initial product data, colors, prices, and SKUs are synthetically generated within a local WordPress/WooCommerce Docker environment during task setup.
